skill.name: gws-gmail-triage
skill.description: Show a read-only Gmail unread inbox summary with sender, subject, date, optional labels, and custom search queries.
skill.summary: Summarize unread Gmail messages through the gws CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backtick execution, path traversal, and weak cryptography are false positives from Markdown examples and relative documentation links. The skill still has medium privacy risk because it instructs authenticated Gmail CLI use that can expose sender, subject, date, and labels to the AI session.

medium_findings.title: Sensitive Gmail Metadata Display
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to show unread Gmail sender, subject, date, and optional labels. This is expected behavior, but it can expose sensitive personal or business email metadata to the AI session or logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly states that the command displays unread inbox summaries and optional labels. The risk is privacy exposure from intended output, not malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External CLI Dependency
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the user to run gws Gmail commands and refers to shared authentication guidance. This is legitimate for a Google Workspace CLI skill, but it depends on a trusted gws installation and valid user authorization.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and the prerequisite references authentication rules. No command injection is present, but external authenticated tooling increases operational risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code, a fenced bash usage block, a flags table, and examples. SKILL.md does not contain Ruby code, shell interpolation, or executable script logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are documentation syntax and command examples. There is no Ruby runtime context or dynamic shell evaluation in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ references point to sibling skill documentation for shared auth rules and related Gmail commands. They are Markdown links and prerequisite text, not file-read code or path construction from user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain relative documentation references. No evidence found of code opening arbitrary paths or accepting user-controlled filesystem input.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Without Cryptographic Use
low_findings.description: The flagged lines contain the skill description and a Markdown table header. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, cipher configuration, hashing, signing, or encryption logic in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines do not contain cryptographic APIs or algorithm names. The file is short documentation, so the absence of crypto use is easy to verify.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Gmail CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples invoke gws gmail +triage, which can read Gmail metadata using the user's configured Google Workspace access. Users should run it only in trusted workspaces and with intended accounts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The CLI invocations are direct and authenticated Gmail access is implied by the prerequisite. The pattern is expected for the skill but carries data exposure risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Email Subject Extraction Pipeline
dangerous_patterns.description: One example pipes JSON output to jq to extract message subjects. This can expose sensitive subject lines outside Gmail into terminal history, logs, or the AI transcript.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact command extracts subject fields from command output. The risk depends on where the terminal output is stored or shared.

content.value_statement: Unread inboxes hide urgent messages and make prioritization slow. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code run a read-only Gmail triage command and summarize what needs attention.
actual_capabilities: Runs the gws Gmail triage command documented by the skill.
Shows unread message sender, subject, and date fields.
Uses Gmail search syntax through the --query option.
Includes label names when --labels is requested.
Supports table output and JSON output through the gws CLI.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI and the shared Google Workspace authentication setup.
Only summarizes messages available to the authenticated Gmail account.
Does not modify, archive, label, or send email.
May expose sensitive email metadata in terminal output or AI transcripts.

use_cases.title: Morning Inbox Review
use_cases.description: List unread messages so a busy professional can identify urgent senders and subjects before starting work.

use_cases.title: Support Queue Scan
use_cases.description: Filter unread mail by a support label or sender query to spot customer issues that need fast action.

use_cases.title: Executive Message Check
use_cases.description: Review a small unread set from priority contacts without changing mailbox state.

prompt_templates.title: Show Unread Inbox
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Gmail triage skill to show my unread inbox summary. Keep the output concise.

prompt_templates.title: Limit Message Count
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Gmail triage for my five most relevant unread messages. Summarize sender, subject, and date.

prompt_templates.title: Filter by Sender
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Gmail triage with a search query for unread messages from my manager. Include only sender, subject, and date.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize With Labels
prompt_templates.prompt: Run Gmail triage for unread work messages, include labels, then group the results by likely urgency without modifying email.

output_examples.output: The result is a short table of unread messages with sender, subject, and date. The assistant can summarize which items look urgent.

output_examples.input: Find unread mail from a specific sender.
output_examples.output: The result is a filtered list of unread matching messages. The assistant can identify the latest message and its subject.
The result includes message labels with each unread item. The assistant can group messages by label for easier review.

best_practices: Use narrow Gmail queries when reviewing sensitive accounts.
Limit result counts with --max before sharing output with an AI assistant.
Confirm the active Google Workspace account before running the command.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad triage on accounts that contain highly sensitive email unless needed.
Do not paste full message bodies into prompts when metadata is enough.
Do not assume the skill can modify Gmail state or send replies.

faq.question: Does this skill change my mailbox?
faq.answer: No. The skill documentation states that it is read-only and does not modify the mailbox.

faq.question: What information does it show?
faq.answer: It shows unread message sender, subject, date, and optional labels.

faq.question: Can I filter the results?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --query option with Gmail search syntax and --max to limit the number of messages.

faq.question: Does it require authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on the gws CLI and the shared Google Workspace authentication setup.

faq.question: Can it summarize message bodies?
faq.answer: No evidence found that this skill reads message bodies. It documents unread inbox summary fields only.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with confidential email?
faq.answer: Use caution. Sender, subject, date, and labels can still contain sensitive information.
